drugs.name: Amoxicillin
drugs.mechanism: Inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis by binding to PBPs (Penicillin Binding Proteins). Causes osmotic lysis of bacteria.

body_pathway.absorption: Well absorbed from small intestine (93% bioavailability)
body_pathway.stomach: Stable in gastric acid unlike ampicillin
body_pathway.distribution: Widely distributed to tissues and fluids
body_pathway.target: Bacterial cell wall in stomach lining
body_pathway.action: Blocks transpeptidase enzyme — bacteria cannot build cell wall — bursts and dies

drugs.key_exam_fact: Part of H. pylori TRIPLE THERAPY: Amoxicillin + Clarithromycin + PPI

drugs.name: Bismuth Compounds
drugs.class: Heavy metal compound / Mucosal protective + Antimicrobial
drugs.mechanism: Disrupts bacterial cell membrane and inhibits urease enzyme of H. pylori. Also forms protective coating on ulcers.

body_pathway.stomach: Reacts with HCl to form bismuth oxychloride and bismuth citrate
body_pathway.action_on_bacteria: Inhibits H. pylori urease, phospholipase, and adhesion to stomach wall
body_pathway.protective_layer: Coats ulcer base with bismuth-protein complex — physical barrier
body_pathway.excretion: Primarily in feces, small amount in urine

drugs.key_exam_fact: Bismuth causes BLACK STOOL — not blood — educate patient!

drug_classes.name: H2 Histamine Receptor Blockers
drug_classes.mechanism_class: Competitively block H2 receptors on gastric parietal cells → decreased cAMP → decreased H+/K+ ATPase activity → 70% reduction in acid secretion

drugs.name: Famotidine
drugs.potency: Most potent H2 blocker (20x more potent than cimetidine)
drugs.mechanism: Selective competitive antagonist of H2 receptors on parietal cells

body_pathway.target: H2 receptors on basolateral membrane of parietal cells
body_pathway.action: Blocks histamine binding → no cAMP increase → proton pump less active → less HCl
body_pathway.stomach_effect: Acid volume and concentration both decrease
body_pathway.duration_effect: Suppresses both basal and stimulated acid secretion

drugs.advantages: No CYP450 inhibition, no anti-androgenic effects
drugs.key_exam_fact: FAMOTIDINE = Most potent H2 blocker with FEWEST drug interactions

drugs.name: Cimetidine
drugs.special_notes: INHIBITS CYP450 enzymes — many drug interactions. Has anti-androgenic effects — causes gynecomastia in men.
drug_interactions: Diazepam — all increased by CYP450 inhibition
drugs.key_exam_fact: CIMETIDINE = CYP450 inhibitor + Gynecomastia — most side effects among H2 blockers

drug_classes.name: Proton Pump Inhibitors
drug_classes.mechanism_class: Prodrugs — converted to active sulfenamide in acid environment — covalently (irreversibly) bind to H+/K+ ATPase (proton pump) — PERMANENTLY disable the pump — new pumps must be synthesized (takes 18-24 hours) — acid secretion suppressed 90-95%
important_notes: All PPIs are PRODRUGS — need acidic environment to activate
Must be taken 30-60 minutes BEFORE meal for best effect
Irreversible binding — effect lasts until new pumps made
Long term use: B12 deficiency, Mg deficiency, C. diff risk, bone fractures

drugs.name: Omeprazole
drugs.key_exam_fact: FIRST PPI ever developed. Metabolized by CYP2C19 — poor metabolizers have higher drug levels

drugs.name: Pantoprazole
drugs.advantage: FEWEST drug interactions among all PPIs
drugs.key_exam_fact: PANTOPRAZOLE = Safest PPI for patients on multiple medications

drugs.name: Esomeprazole
drugs.note: S-isomer of omeprazole, more consistent acid suppression
drugs.key_exam_fact: Purple pill — S-isomer of omeprazole — longer lasting effect

drug_classes.name: Antacids
drug_classes.mechanism_class: Direct chemical neutralization of HCl already in stomach — no effect on acid production — only neutralize existing acid

drugs.name: Magnesium Hydroxide
drugs.key_exam_fact: Mg antacids = DIARRHEA. Combined with Al antacids to balance effects.

drugs.name: Aluminum Hydroxide
drugs.key_exam_fact: Al antacids = CONSTIPATION + Phosphate depletion in kidneys

drugs.name: Calcium Carbonate
drugs.warning: ACID REBOUND — calcium stimulates gastrin release → more acid later
drugs.key_exam_fact: Calcium carbonate = ACID REBOUND effect + CO2 causes belching

drugs.name: Sodium Bicarbonate
drugs.warning: SYSTEMIC ALKALOSIS risk — absorbed into blood — avoid in kidney/heart patients
drugs.key_exam_fact: Fastest antacid but SYSTEMIC absorption = metabolic alkalosis risk

drugs.name: Sucralfate
drugs.mechanism: In acidic pH → polymerizes → forms viscous gel → binds to ulcer base (positively charged bismuth binds negatively charged proteins at ulcer) → physical barrier → protects from acid/pepsin/bile
drugs.important: NEEDS ACID to work — do NOT give with PPIs or antacids at same time
drugs.key_exam_fact: Sucralfate works ONLY in acidic environment — never give with PPIs simultaneously

drugs.name: Bismuth Subsalicylate
drugs.key_exam_fact: Used in QUADRUPLE THERAPY for H. pylori: Bismuth + Metronidazole + Tetracycline + PPI

drugs.name: Misoprostol
drugs.mechanism: Binds EP3 receptors on parietal cells → decreases cAMP → less acid. Binds EP4 on mucous cells → increases mucus and bicarbonate → protects lining.
side_effects: CONTRAINDICATED IN PREGNANCY — causes uterine contractions
drugs.key_exam_fact: Misoprostol = Given WITH NSAIDs to protect stomach. CONTRAINDICATED in pregnancy.

drugs.name: Dicyclomine
drugs.mechanism: Blocks M3 muscarinic receptors on GI smooth muscle → prevents acetylcholine binding → smooth muscle relaxation → reduces spasm and cramping
drugs.use: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS) — reduces pain and cramping
drugs.memory_trick: Anti-MUSCARINIC = Anti-MOISTURE = Dry mouth, Dry eyes, Constipation
drugs.key_exam_fact: Dicyclomine for IBS. Side effects = all DRY: dry mouth, dry eyes, urinary retention
